milestones.vision: Lock the Cubase-first VST3 model, establish poly_engine isolation boundary, and prove deterministic timing before any feature work. An empty instrument loads in Cubase, the engine compiles without the VST3 SDK, and golden tests verify byte-identical output under transport stress.

poly_engine compiles and passes tests with no VST3 SDK dependency

key_risks.risk: VST3 note output and event routing semantics may not work as expected for MIDI-only instrument in Cubase
key_risks.whyItMatters: Cubase expects instruments to have a silent audio output bus; incorrect event routing would block all downstream work

key_risks.risk: Envelope phase drift across process blocks and loop boundaries
key_risks.whyItMatters: Determinism is a core product principle — if timing accumulates rather than deriving from absolute PPQ, output varies across loop restarts and the engine is fundamentally broken

key_risks.risk: CMake integration with Steinberg VST3 SDK may have undocumented constraints
key_risks.whyItMatters: The SDK ships its own CMake helpers (smtg_add_vst3plugin) that may conflict with project-level CMake conventions or C++20 flags

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: VST3 MIDI-only instrument routing in Cubase
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Empty VST3 with silent audio bus loads and routes correctly in Cubase
Golden tests produce byte-identical event streams across loop restart, tempo ramp, and position jump scenarios
poly_engine static library compiles and runs its test suite with zero VST3 SDK headers or libraries

milestones.title: Core Groove Engine MVP
milestones.vision: A musically useful polymetric groove generator with no ML. Lane-independent Euclidean rhythms with dynamics-first velocity shaping, superimposed multi-period envelopes, coherent macro controls, real-time-safe VST3 plugin integration, and a minimal but legible VSTGUI editor. The plugin is stable under start/stop, loop, and tempo changes in Cubase.
success_criteria: Stable under start/stop, loop, and tempo change in Cubase
Visible lane-to-audio correspondence in the UI
Audible velocity and emphasis variation across lanes
Patch save/load round-trips correctly with version stamp
No heap allocation, locks, exceptions, or I/O in process()

key_risks.risk: Envelope-lane interaction complexity when multiple envelopes modulate same target
key_risks.whyItMatters: Stacking envelopes on velocity or density could produce degenerate output (all silent, all max) if combination logic is not carefully bounded

key_risks.risk: Real-time safety in VST3 process() with full engine running
key_risks.whyItMatters: Engine pre-allocation strategy must handle worst-case 8 lanes with multiple envelopes without any dynamic allocation in the audio callback
First VSTGUI project for the portfolio; editor development may take longer than estimated and block validation of the full plugin experience
If macros map as simple multiplies rather than coherent musical transformations, they will feel like generic knobs rather than compositional tools

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Envelope stacking produces musically valid output
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Golden tests verify bounded, non-degenerate output when multiple envelopes target the same parameter simultaneously

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: process() is real-time safe under full load
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Debug allocator hook confirms zero allocations in renderRange(); stress test with 8 lanes and max envelopes passes

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Macro mapping feels musical, not mechanical
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Macro sweep golden tests show non-linear, musically coherent parameter evolution across the range

milestones.title: Musical Refinement and Cubase Polish
milestones.vision: Turn the groove generator into a composition tool. Extended envelope system with full target set, scene/snapshot switching with morph, structural constraints that protect musical anchors, polished Cubase automation mapping, MIDI export workflow for composition integration, and phase/envelope interaction visualizations. The user can write a full rhythmic section while keeping structural anchors intact.
success_criteria: User can write a full rhythmic section while keeping structural anchors intact
Multiple simultaneous envelopes per lane with full target set functional
Scene A/B switching and morph produces smooth musical transitions
Constraints protect structural elements (kick anchor, backbeat, density ranges)
MIDI capture/drag-to-track/freeze workflow functional in Cubase
Phase and envelope visualizations help users understand evolving polymetric structure

key_risks.risk: Scene morph between arbitrary GrooveStates may produce non-musical intermediate states
key_risks.whyItMatters: Interpolating between two groove configurations could create transient rhythmic chaos unless the morph path is carefully constrained

key_risks.risk: Constraint interactions with envelope modulation create conflicting demands
key_risks.whyItMatters: An envelope pushing density down while a constraint demands minimum density creates a contradiction that needs a clear resolution policy

key_risks.risk: MIDI drag-to-track and freeze workflows depend on undocumented Cubase VST3 extension behavior
key_risks.whyItMatters: Cubase MIDI export interop may require reverse-engineering host-specific APIs not covered by the VST3 standard

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Morph between divergent scenes produces bounded, non-degenerate intermediate states in listening tests
Resolution policy for conflicting constraint/envelope demands produces predictable, audible results
At least one of drag-to-track or pattern export works in Cubase without host-specific hacks

# S01: Build System and VST3 Skeleton

**CMake build system with VST3 SDK FetchContent, poly_engine static lib, and poly_plugin VST3 instrument skeleton producing a clean .vst3 bundle**

## What Happened

Established the full build infrastructure for Poly. The top-level CMakeLists.txt fetches the Steinberg VST3 SDK v3.7.12 via FetchContent, sets C++20, and includes the shared jk_warnings.cmake module (extended to suppress SDK deprecation warnings). poly_engine is a standalone STATIC library with no VST3 SDK dependency — it defines TransportContext, NoteEvent, NoteEventBuffer (pre-allocated for RT safety), and the Engine::renderRange() contract. poly_plugin uses smtg_add_vst3plugin with factory registration (kInstrumentSynth), PolyProcessor (AudioEffect with stereo audio out + MIDI event out, versioned state), and PolyController stub. Platform entry points (macmain.cpp/dllmain.cpp/linuxmain.cpp) are included per-platform. The build produces a valid .vst3 bundle with zero warnings from project source files.

## Verification

Clean build with zero project warnings, .vst3 bundle at build/VST3/Debug/poly_plugin.vst3, engine isolation confirmed via grep (no VST3 SDK references in engine/)

# S03: Timing Determinism Prototype

**Engine produces deterministic polymetric output proven by 18 tests covering block-size independence, loop restart, position jump, and tempo invariance**

## What Happened

Implemented the core timing engine in four tasks: (T01) Euclidean rhythm algorithm with Bresenham distribution and rotation, (T02) PPQ-based absolute-position cycle timing in renderRange(), (T03) position-seeded deterministic RNG for probability gating and velocity spread, (T04) 8-test golden determinism suite plus a checked-in reference file. The engine is a pure function of (patch, seed, transport position) with zero accumulated state. All phase computation derives from absolute PPQ — no drift under loop restarts, tempo changes, or position jumps. The polymetric 5/16 ghost lane correctly shows phase variation against the 4/4 grid.

## Verification

18/18 tests pass: 10 Euclidean algorithm tests + 8 golden determinism tests. Block-size independence verified across 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0 PPQ. Harness produces audible polymetric output (79 events over 4 bars).

# T02: Implemented core cycle timing in renderRange() with PPQ-based absolute positioning

**Implemented core cycle timing in renderRange() with PPQ-based absolute positioning**

## What Happened

Implemented the core rendering loop in Engine::renderRange(). For each active lane: computes step PPQ from subdivision, maps absolute step indices into the Euclidean pattern via cycle modulo, and emits NoteEvents for hits falling within [ppqStart, ppqEnd). All phase computation derives from absolute PPQ position — no accumulators. Added deterministicRand() in rng.h using splitmix64-style hashing keyed on (seed, laneId, absStep, channel). Channel 0 drives probability gating, channel 1 drives velocity spread.
